ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steve Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Diane Gentry for Valentino Fazzari. Richard Pierce for Vincent Fazzari. Lynne Searle for (creditor) Robert Coen.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that they filed an accounting and that they have concerns regarding taxes. Ms. Gentry stated that they have been working with a CPA regarding tax liability and that they have filed a tax relief request. Mr. Pierce stated that the alternative is a petition to terminate, that there is nothing in the estate, that mistakes were made, that Valentino handled everything and that Vincent was out of the country from 1993-2008 and that there is a pending probate in Nevada in regards to two old Rolls Royce vehicles. Ms. Searle stated that in regards to the termination issue, other creditors have been dealt with, that it is assumed that Mr. Coen's claim is allowed but they are unable to determine if that is so, that it needs to be allowed here and cited 9251 that the court needs to allow or reject the claim. Mr. Weinkauf stated that there are no assets, that he does not know why the court was not notified earlier and that he did not know about the creditors. Ms. Gentry stated that there is nothing in the estate and that they are asking that no accounting be required. Ms. Searle stated that then they will not have the allowance they are looking for and that the statute requires the clerk to present the claim to the court. The Court stated that it is inclined to terminate but that it does not want consequences. Ms. Searle stated that the court writes on the claim itself whether it is allowed or rejected. Ms. Gentry stated that they want clarification, that if the estate is terminated where will Ms. Searle be looking to for payment of the claim. Ms. Searle stated that the claim can be presented to the trustees if there is no money in the estate but that it must be allowed in the estate case. Mr. Weinkauf asked how the Public Administrator is to get its fees. Ms. Gentry stated that the brothers did not understand what was going on. Mr. Weinkauf stated that the Public Administrator does not want to be responsible for their tax issues. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kinnear. (Not Reported)

Petition By Personal Representative For Leave To Resign
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Continued To Dec-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diane Gentry for Valentino Fazzari. Sally Gratz for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). John Leutza for (creditor) R.J. Coen. Richard Pierce for Vincent Fazzari.

The Court asked Ms. Gratz if they needed to amend the petition to terminate for lack of assets. Ms. Gratz stated that they are asking to resign and to have the original administrator reinstated, that they have collected no assets and have made no disbursements, that they have made many appearances and that they do not want to be held liable. The Court asked if all of the parties were on the same page regarding the lack of assets and why not terminate. Ms. Gratz stated that if they do a final accounting and wrap up they could be held liable, that they do not have the necessary documents, that they know nothing about the taxes and that they are only here as a neutral stakeholder. Mr. Leutza stated that he only represents a creditor and that they are waiting for payment. Ms. Gentry stated that they would prefer that the administrator be reinstated, that there is an issue as to how the debt to R.J. Coen will be paid and that taxes have been paid. Mr. Pierce stated that they do not believe that the asset which is part of the trust can be paid from the estate, that the 2012 Heggstad petition put the asset into the trust and that the only assets, which he has inspected and Valentino is correct in his statement are 2 old Rolls Royces in bad condition. Ms. Gratz stated that the SFPA wants to paid for its services. Ms. Gentry stated that she objects, that as of January of 2012 the assets were in the trust, that the examiner required her client to do an exhaustive document request which was costly, that they had to file an additional 850 petition due to an examiner error, that the SFPA says it did no work and that the appointment of the SFPA was not due to any wrong doing of her client. Mr. Pierce stated that it would be prudent to see what the fees are. The Court stated that the fees are not before it, that the SFPA is to amend the petition to terminate due to lack of assets, that it is not going to reinstate the previous administrator and that the amended petition is to be filed by December 21, 2015. The petition by personal representative for leave to resign and the 3 probate status reports were continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
